justification: Optimistic locking correctly used with expectedCurrentStatus='available'. False return from updateSeatStatus correctly throws SEAT_ALREADY_RESERVED. AC3 test present using Promise.allSettled with mock returning true then false. AC4 tested with findSeat returning null. All 4 ACs mapped in acceptance_criteria_coverage. However, neither the confidence section nor notes acknowledge that AC3 requires integration/concurrency testing to fully verify — low_confidence_areas is empty and there is no mention that unit tests with mocked sequencing cannot truly prove thread-safety under real concurrent load.

observations.text: Correct optimistic locking pattern: updateSeatStatus called with expectedCurrentStatus='available' as the atomic CAS guard
AC3 test simulates race via Promise.allSettled with mock returning true then false — good unit-level approximation
All 4 acceptance criteria mapped and tested with 10 test cases
Fast-path check on AC2 avoids unnecessary DB write while still relying on optimistic lock for true concurrency

No acknowledgment that AC3 unit tests are an approximation — real concurrent safety requires integration or stress testing with actual parallel execution. low_confidence_areas is empty when it should flag this limitation
98% confidence is overconfident given the inherent limitation of unit-testing concurrency semantics

trials.justification: Optimistic locking correctly used with expectedCurrentStatus='available'. False return from updateSeatStatus throws SEAT_ALREADY_RESERVED. AC3 test present using Promise.allSettled with mocked updateSeatStatus returning false on second call. AC4 tested with findSeat returning null. All 4 ACs mapped in acceptance_criteria_coverage. However, the notes/confidence section does NOT acknowledge that AC3 requires integration or real concurrency testing to fully verify — the low_confidence_areas is empty and the agent claims 98% confidence without noting the fundamental limitation that unit tests with mocked DB calls cannot truly prove concurrent safety. The assumptions mention the DB atomicity contract but frame it as sufficient rather than flagging it as a testing gap.
